column1: B.  DETAILED ANGLE CONVENTIONS  (as used in Arm_Lib and Jupyter notebooks)

column2: Convention / Notes
90° = camera-side of robot facing front (centered). 0° = arm rotated to left. 180° = arm rotated to right. Increasing angle = clockwise from above.

90° = arm segment pointing straight up. 0° = segment horizontal, extending away from robot. 180° = segment horizontal, extending toward/behind robot. INVERTED: Arm_Lib angle 90° sends raw position 2000 (servo midpoint) after inversion.
Same convention as Servo 2. 90° = straight up relative to prior segment. 0° = extends away. 180° = folds back. INVERTED.
Same convention as Servo 2 and 3. 90° = continuation of prior segment direction. INVERTED.

0° = gripper servo body facing right. 45° = fingers angled down-left / up-right. 90° = servo facing down, fingers parallel left/right. 135° = fingers down-right / up-left. 270° = servo facing up.  NOT INVERTED.  Range: 0–270°.
0–50° = fully open to partially open (opening out phase). 51–170° = closing. ~170° = fully closed. Practical range 0–50 for open, ~130–170 for gripping.  NOT INVERTED.
S1 (Base)

column9: Curled tight over base, gripper half open.

column1: D.  PORTING NOTES — Arm_Lib (I2C/expansion board)  ?  Arm_Driver (direct bus servo via USB)

column2: Arm_Driver equivalent
Convert angle?raw pos (apply inversion for IDs 2,3,4). Send CMD 0x03 / ADDR 0x2A packet to servo ID.
Send 6 individual CMD 0x03 / ADDR 0x2A packets with per-servo position in sequence.
column6: No broadcast equivalent for different positions. Send one packet per servo.

column2: Same as above — unpack array, apply per-servo conversion, send 6 packets.
Send CMD 0x02 / ADDR 0x38 packet. Read reply (discard echo if using resistor hack). Convert raw pos ? angle (inverse of write conversion).
column6: Same return value: angle in degrees or None on failure.

column2: Send CMD 0x03 / ADDR 0x28 with data=0x01 (enable) or 0x00 (disable) to each servo ID 1–6.
column6: Yahboom register 0x28 = torque enable on YB-SD15M.

column2: Send CMD 0x03 / ADDR 0x05 packet with ID=0xFE (broadcast). new_id = target value.
column6: One servo connected at a time only.

column1: F.  ADDITIONAL API FUNCTIONS (from Yahboom PDF) — Porting Notes

column3:
Same read packet as Arm_serial_servo_read:
CMD 0x02 / ADDR 0x38 to target ID.
Convert raw pos to degrees.
No inversion applied (caller responsible for physical mapping outside IDs 1-6).
column8: Useful for bench testing spares or replacement servos without reassigning IDs to 1-6.

column3:
Send read packet (CMD 0x02 / ADDR 0x38) and check reply arrives within timeout.
If reply checksum valid: return DATA_H byte (0xDA = healthy).
If no reply or checksum fail: return 0x00.
Note: same packet as position read — servo embeds status in reply byte.
column8:
Use for startup self-check.
Ping IDs 1-6 before enabling motion.
Log any non-0xDA response.
For all-clear, all 6 must return 0xDA.

column1:
Arm_serial_servo_write(id=0, angle, time)
id=0 broadcasts same angle to all 6 servos
column3:
Use broadcast ID 0xFE in packet header.
Applies same raw pos to ALL servos — no per-servo inversion.
For inversion-aware all-same-angle, send 6 individual packets with adjusted angles for IDs 2,3,4.
column8:
Broadcast does NOT trigger servo replies.
Primary use: homing all servos to 90° simultaneously.
For different angles per servo, use write6 or write6_array instead.
